title: Vaughania xerophila
intro: Vaughania xerophila, also known as Bhangarayan or Bhangarayan root, is a plant species native to India and other parts of Asia, belonging to the family Cucurbitaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antidiabetic activities, as well as potential uses in treating various infections and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of Vaughania xerophila include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Ayurvedic medicine, Vaughania xerophila is commonly prepared into decoctions, powders, and oils, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, cough, and skin disorders.
